description: Five entries whose title and section labels are whole-line **bold** form with no ATX heading markers anywhere — exercises the bold heading marker.

entries.content:
**2026-07-20 — notifier: delayed pages**

**What happened**
Paging notifications lagged by up to six minutes during a burst of alerts.

**Timeline**
At 10:02 the burst began. 10:05 triage began. 10:11 the queue worker count was increased. 10:14 lag cleared.

**Resolution**
Scaled up the notifier queue workers to absorb the burst.

**Follow-ups**
Add autoscaling to the notifier queue workers so a burst does not need a manual scale-up.

**2026-07-16 — checkout: duplicate charges**

**What happened**
A retry storm in the checkout client caused a subset of requests to be charged twice.

**Timeline**
At 15:40 duplicate-charge reports began. 15:44 triage began. 15:58 the retry storm was traced to a missing idempotency key. 16:05 the fix shipped.

**Resolution**
Added an idempotency key to the checkout charge call so retries can no longer double-charge.

**Follow-ups**
Add a test that replays a charge request twice and asserts only one charge lands.

**2026-07-09 — search-api: timeout spike**

**What happened**
search-api request timeouts spiked during a period of unusually high query volume.

**Timeline**
At 13:10 the timeout spike began. 13:13 triage began. 13:22 the query cache was warmed. 13:26 timeouts returned to baseline.

**Resolution**
Warmed the query cache manually; the automatic warmer had not kept pace with the volume increase.

**Follow-ups**
Tune the query cache warmer's schedule to match observed traffic patterns.

**2026-07-02 — payments-api: webhook backlog**

**What happened**
Outbound webhook delivery from payments-api fell behind by roughly forty minutes.

**Timeline**
At 07:15 the backlog was first noticed. 07:20 triage began. 07:35 an extra delivery worker was started. 07:55 the backlog cleared.

**Resolution**
Started an additional webhook delivery worker to drain the backlog.

**Follow-ups**
Alert on webhook delivery lag directly instead of relying on a downstream report.

**2026-06-25 — inventory: sync gap**

**What happened**
inventory counts drifted from the source system for about thirty minutes after a sync job crashed.

**Timeline**
At 09:50 drift was detected. 09:55 triage began. 10:12 the sync job was restarted. 10:20 counts matched again.

**Resolution**
Restarted the crashed sync job; it had run out of memory on a large batch.

**Follow-ups**
Cap the sync job's batch size so a single large batch cannot exhaust its memory again.
